summary: Automated Microservice Testing Pipeline — GitHub Actions CI
description:
Establish a layered automated testing pipeline that runs on every push to GitHub. Currently the only GitHub Actions workflow is pr-checks.yml, which validates PR title format only. All 7 backend services have contextLoads() tests only — no unit tests, no slice tests, no integration tests, and no coverage enforcement.

This epic introduces four test strategies aligned to the test pyramid, wired into a GitHub Actions CI workflow: unit tests (Mockito, no Spring context), controller slice tests (@WebMvcTest), repository slice tests (@DataJpaTest with H2), and full integration tests (@SpringBootTest with H2). JaCoCo is added to loanflow-parent so coverage is enforced uniformly across all services.

loanflow-common must be installed before any service can compile. The CI workflow accounts for this with a build-common job that runs first and caches the ~/.m2 directory for downstream matrix jobs.

loan-service and underwriting-service already have application-test.yaml configured with H2 in MySQL-compatibility mode and Flyway enabled. These are the first services to receive full test coverage. Other services receive context-load tests plus @WebMvcTest slices as controllers are added.
userType: backend developer working across all services in the platform
functionality: automate build, test, and coverage enforcement for all microservices on every push so regressions are caught before code is reviewed or merged
benefit: so broken builds and test failures are surfaced immediately on push rather than discovered manually, and coverage gates prevent untested code from reaching main

stories.summary: Create GitHub Actions CI workflow with matrix build per service
stories.description:
Create .github/workflows/ci.yml that triggers on push to any branch and on pull requests to main or any LF3-* epic branch. The workflow has three jobs:

1. build-common — checks out the repo, installs loanflow-common into the local Maven cache, and uploads the ~/.m2 directory as a cache artifact keyed on the loanflow-parent and loanflow-common pom.xml hashes.

2. unit-and-slice — runs after build-common. Uses a matrix over all 7 services. Each matrix job restores the Maven cache, then runs ./mvnw test -Dspring.profiles.active=test. Runs on every push to every branch.

3. integration — runs after build-common, only on pull_request events. Matrix over loan-service and underwriting-service only (the two services with H2 test infrastructure). Runs ./mvnw verify -Dspring.profiles.active=test.

Services without database dependencies (identity-service, gateway-service, document-service, notification-service, discovery-server) pass through the unit-and-slice job using their existing contextLoads() tests. They will gain slice tests as controllers are implemented.

acceptanceCriteria.scenario: Push to any branch triggers unit-and-slice job for all services
acceptanceCriteria.given: a developer pushes a commit to any branch
acceptanceCriteria.then: the build-common job runs first and all 7 unit-and-slice matrix jobs run in parallel after it completes

acceptanceCriteria.scenario: Pull request triggers integration job for DB-backed services
acceptanceCriteria.given: a developer opens or pushes to a pull request targeting main or an LF3-* branch
acceptanceCriteria.then: the integration matrix job runs for loan-service and underwriting-service in addition to the unit-and-slice jobs

acceptanceCriteria.scenario: A failing test in one matrix service does not suppress the failure
acceptanceCriteria.given: a test in loan-service fails
acceptanceCriteria.then: the loan-service matrix job is marked failed and the overall workflow fails, while the other service jobs still run to completion

acceptanceCriteria.scenario: Maven cache is restored from a prior run
acceptanceCriteria.given: the pom.xml files for loanflow-parent and loanflow-common have not changed since the last run
acceptanceCriteria.then: the cache restore step reports a cache hit and no Maven dependencies are re-downloaded

tasks.description:
Create .github/workflows/ci.yml with the following structure:

```yaml
name: CI

on:
  push:
    branches: ['**']
  pull_request:
    branches: [main, 'LF3-*']

jobs:
  build-common:
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    steps:
      - uses: actions/checkout@v4
      - uses: actions/setup-java@v4
        with:
          java-version: '17'
          distribution: 'temurin'
      - uses: actions/cache@v4
        with:
          path: ~/.m2/repository
          key: m2-common-${{ hashFiles('backend/loanflow-parent/pom.xml', 'backend/loanflow-common/pom.xml') }}
          restore-keys: m2-common-
      - name: Install loanflow-common
        run: cd backend/loanflow-common && ./mvnw clean install -DskipTests

  unit-and-slice:
    needs: build-common
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    strategy:
      matrix:
        service:
          - loan-service
          - identity-service
          - gateway-service
          - document-service
          - underwriting-service
          - notification-service
          - discovery-server
    steps:
      - uses: actions/checkout@v4
      - uses: actions/setup-java@v4
        with:
          java-version: '17'
          distribution: 'temurin'
      - uses: actions/cache@v4
        with:
          path: ~/.m2/repository
          key: m2-common-${{ hashFiles('backend/loanflow-parent/pom.xml', 'backend/loanflow-common/pom.xml') }}
          restore-keys: m2-common-
      - name: Test ${{ matrix.service }}
        run: cd backend/${{ matrix.service }} && ./mvnw test -Dspring.profiles.active=test

  integration:
    needs: build-common
    if: github.event_name == 'pull_request'
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    strategy:
      matrix:
        service:
          - loan-service
          - underwriting-service
    steps:
      - uses: actions/checkout@v4
      - uses: actions/setup-java@v4
        with:
          java-version: '17'
          distribution: 'temurin'
      - uses: actions/cache@v4
        with:
          path: ~/.m2/repository
          key: m2-common-${{ hashFiles('backend/loanflow-parent/pom.xml', 'backend/loanflow-common/pom.xml') }}
          restore-keys: m2-common-
      - name: Verify ${{ matrix.service }}
        run: cd backend/${{ matrix.service }} && ./mvnw verify -Dspring.profiles.active=test
```

stories.summary: Add JaCoCo code coverage enforcement to loanflow-parent
stories.description:
Add the JaCoCo Maven plugin to backend/loanflow-parent/pom.xml so all services inherit it without declaring it themselves. Configure three executions: prepare-agent (instrument bytecode), report (generate HTML/XML report in target/site/jacoco), and check (fail the build when line coverage is below the minimum threshold).

Set the initial threshold to 0.50 (50%) — a deliberately low bar that the existing contextLoads() tests already exceed — so the build does not break immediately. The threshold is raised incrementally as each story in this epic adds real tests. Coverage reports are uploaded as GitHub Actions artifacts so they are visible from the workflow run summary.

acceptanceCriteria.scenario: mvnw verify generates a JaCoCo coverage report
acceptanceCriteria.given: JaCoCo is configured in loanflow-parent and mvnw verify is run in loan-service
acceptanceCriteria.then: a coverage report is generated at target/site/jacoco/index.html

acceptanceCriteria.scenario: Build fails when line coverage is below the configured threshold
acceptanceCriteria.given: JaCoCo check is configured with minimum=0.50 and actual line coverage is 0.30
acceptanceCriteria.then: the check execution fails the build with a coverage threshold violation message

acceptanceCriteria.scenario: JaCoCo report is uploaded as a CI artifact
acceptanceCriteria.given: the integration job runs to completion for loan-service
acceptanceCriteria.then: a JaCoCo HTML report is available as a downloadable artifact in the GitHub Actions run summary

tasks.summary: Add JaCoCo plugin to loanflow-parent pom.xml
tasks.description:
Add the following plugin under <build><plugins> in backend/loanflow-parent/pom.xml:

```xml
<plugin>
    <groupId>org.jacoco</groupId>
    <artifactId>jacoco-maven-plugin</artifactId>
    <version>0.8.11</version>
    <executions>
        <execution>
            <id>prepare-agent</id>
            <goals><goal>prepare-agent</goal></goals>
        </execution>
        <execution>
            <id>report</id>
            <phase>test</phase>
            <goals><goal>report</goal></goals>
        </execution>
        <execution>
            <id>check</id>
            <goals><goal>check</goal></goals>
            <configuration>
                <rules>
                    <rule>
                        <limits>
                            <limit>
                                <counter>LINE</counter>
                                <value>COVEREDRATIO</value>
                                <minimum>0.50</minimum>
                            </limit>
                        </limits>
                    </rule>
                </rules>
            </configuration>
        </execution>
    </executions>
</plugin>
```

Run mvnw verify -DskipTests from backend/loan-service to confirm the plugin is inherited and no build errors occur.

tasks.summary: Upload JaCoCo report as GitHub Actions artifact in ci.yml
tasks.description:
Add the following step to the integration job in .github/workflows/ci.yml, after the verify step:

```yaml
- name: Upload coverage report
  if: always()
  uses: actions/upload-artifact@v4
  with:
    name: jacoco-${{ matrix.service }}
    path: backend/${{ matrix.service }}/target/site/jacoco/
    retention-days: 7
```

The if: always() condition ensures the report is uploaded even when the coverage check fails, so the developer can inspect actual coverage before fixing the threshold gap.

stories.summary: Add unit tests for LoanApplicationServiceImpl using Mockito
stories.description:
Write pure unit tests for com.loanflow.loanservice.service.impl.LoanApplicationServiceImpl. No Spring context is loaded — use @ExtendWith(MockitoExtension.class) and @Mock LoanApplicationRepository. Tests run in the unit-and-slice CI job on every push.

Cover the two methods added in the get-application-details epic: createApplication (stub) and getApplicationById. Focus on business logic branches: missing application (ResourceNotFoundException), ownership violation for APPLICANT role (AccessDeniedException), successful retrieval for APPLICANT owner, and successful retrieval for LOAN_OFFICER regardless of ownership.

acceptanceCriteria.scenario: Unit test suite runs without starting a Spring context
acceptanceCriteria.given: LoanApplicationServiceImplTest is annotated with @ExtendWith(MockitoExtension.class)
acceptanceCriteria.then: all tests pass and no Spring application context is created during the test run

acceptanceCriteria.scenario: getApplicationById throws ResourceNotFoundException when repository returns empty
acceptanceCriteria.given: loanApplicationRepository.findById returns Optional.empty()
acceptanceCriteria.then: ResourceNotFoundException is thrown with a message containing the applicationId

acceptanceCriteria.scenario: getApplicationById throws AccessDeniedException when APPLICANT requests another user's application
acceptanceCriteria.given: the repository returns a LoanApplication with applicantUserId=10 and callerUserId=99 with role APPLICANT
acceptanceCriteria.then: AccessDeniedException is thrown and the response is never built

acceptanceCriteria.scenario: getApplicationById returns response for the owning APPLICANT
acceptanceCriteria.given: the repository returns a LoanApplication with applicantUserId=10 and callerUserId=10 with role APPLICANT
acceptanceCriteria.then: an ApplicationDetailResponse is returned with applicationId, status, loanType, amount, termMonths, and annualIncome matching the entity

acceptanceCriteria.scenario: getApplicationById skips ownership check for LOAN_OFFICER
acceptanceCriteria.given: the repository returns a LoanApplication with applicantUserId=10 and callerUserId=99 with role LOAN_OFFICER
acceptanceCriteria.then: an ApplicationDetailResponse is returned without AccessDeniedException

stories.summary: Add @WebMvcTest controller slice tests for LoanApplicationController
stories.description:
Write controller slice tests using @WebMvcTest for com.loanflow.loanservice.controller.LoanApplicationController. MockMvc exercises the full HTTP layer including Spring Security, request mapping, @Valid enforcement, and response serialisation, without loading JPA or Flyway.

The service layer is replaced with a @MockBean. JWT authentication is simulated using Spring Security Test's @WithMockUser or by injecting a mock JWT via SecurityMockMvcRequestPostProcessors.jwt().

Test both the POST /api/v1/applications endpoint from the create epic and the GET /api/v1/applications/{applicationId} endpoint from the get-details epic.

acceptanceCriteria.scenario: Controller test loads only the web layer without JPA or Flyway
acceptanceCriteria.given: LoanApplicationControllerTest is annotated with @WebMvcTest(LoanApplicationController.class)
acceptanceCriteria.then: no DataSource, EntityManager, or Flyway bean is created in the test context

acceptanceCriteria.scenario: POST /api/v1/applications without JWT returns 401
acceptanceCriteria.given: no Authorization header is included in the request
acceptanceCriteria.then: the response status is 401

acceptanceCriteria.scenario: POST /api/v1/applications with LOAN_OFFICER role returns 403
acceptanceCriteria.given: the JWT carries role LOAN_OFFICER
acceptanceCriteria.when: MockMvc performs POST /api/v1/applications with a valid request body
acceptanceCriteria.then: the response status is 403

acceptanceCriteria.scenario: POST /api/v1/applications with null loanType returns 400
acceptanceCriteria.given: the JWT carries role APPLICANT and the request body has a null loanType
acceptanceCriteria.then: the response status is 400 and the body contains the ApiErrorResponse shape

acceptanceCriteria.scenario: GET /api/v1/applications/{id} with APPLICANT JWT returns 200 with mocked response
acceptanceCriteria.given: the service mock returns an ApplicationDetailResponse and the JWT carries role APPLICANT
acceptanceCriteria.then: the response status is 200 and the body contains applicationId 50021

tasks.summary: Add spring-security-test dependency to loan-service pom.xml
tasks.description:
Add the following to backend/loan-service/pom.xml under <dependencies>. Scope is test. No version — managed by spring-boot-starter-parent:

```xml
<dependency>
    <groupId>org.springframework.security</groupId>
    <artifactId>spring-security-test</artifactId>
    <scope>test</scope>
</dependency>
```

This provides SecurityMockMvcRequestPostProcessors.jwt() for building mock JWT tokens in slice tests.

stories.summary: Add @DataJpaTest repository slice tests for LoanApplicationRepository
stories.description:
Write JPA slice tests using @DataJpaTest for com.loanflow.loanservice.repository.LoanApplicationRepository. Spring loads only the JPA layer — no web, no service, no Kafka. Flyway runs migrations against H2 (already configured in application-test.yaml). Tests verify the custom query methods that the service layer depends on.

Use @ActiveProfiles("test") so the H2 datasource config in application-test.yaml is picked up automatically.

acceptanceCriteria.scenario: Repository test loads only the JPA layer
acceptanceCriteria.given: LoanApplicationRepositoryTest is annotated with @DataJpaTest
acceptanceCriteria.then: no web layer, service beans, or Kafka configuration is created in the test context

acceptanceCriteria.scenario: findByApplicationReference returns empty Optional for unknown reference
acceptanceCriteria.given: no LoanApplication exists with the given reference
acceptanceCriteria.then: Optional.empty() is returned

acceptanceCriteria.scenario: findByApplicantUserId returns all applications for a given user
acceptanceCriteria.given: two LoanApplications exist with applicantUserId=10 and one with applicantUserId=99
acceptanceCriteria.then: a list of exactly 2 applications is returned

acceptanceCriteria.scenario: existsByApplicantUserIdAndLoanTypeAndStatusIn returns true for an active application
acceptanceCriteria.given: a LoanApplication exists with applicantUserId=10, loanType=PERSONAL, status=SUBMITTED
acceptanceCriteria.then: true is returned

stories.summary: Add @SpringBootTest integration test for loan-service POST and GET flows
stories.description:
Write a full-context integration test for loan-service using @SpringBootTest with a random server port. The full Spring context loads — including JPA, Flyway, and the security filter chain — against the H2 in-memory database configured in application-test.yaml. TestRestTemplate or MockMvc exercises the complete HTTP-to-database path.

This test is the only one that runs in the integration CI job (on pull_request only). It verifies the end-to-end POST /api/v1/applications and GET /api/v1/applications/{id} flows through all layers.

JWT tokens are generated locally using a HMAC secret defined in application-test.yaml (matching the dev profile secret) so no identity-service is required.

acceptanceCriteria.scenario: Full Spring context loads against H2 without errors
acceptanceCriteria.given: LoanServiceIntegrationTest is annotated with @SpringBootTest and @ActiveProfiles('test')
acceptanceCriteria.then: the Spring context starts successfully with H2 as the datasource and all Flyway migrations applied

acceptanceCriteria.scenario: POST /api/v1/applications with valid APPLICANT JWT persists and returns 201
acceptanceCriteria.given: a valid APPLICANT JWT and a valid CreateLoanApplicationRequest body
acceptanceCriteria.then: the response status is 201 and the body contains applicationId, status SUBMITTED, and nextStep UPLOAD_REQUIRED_DOCUMENTS

acceptanceCriteria.scenario: GET /api/v1/applications/{id} retrieves the previously created application
acceptanceCriteria.given: an application was created via POST and its applicationId is known
acceptanceCriteria.when: GET /api/v1/applications/{id} is called with the same APPLICANT JWT
acceptanceCriteria.then: the response status is 200 and the body contains the matching applicationId and status

acceptanceCriteria.scenario: GET /api/v1/applications/{id} returns 404 for an unknown id
acceptanceCriteria.given: no application exists with id 99999
acceptanceCriteria.when: GET /api/v1/applications/99999 is called with a valid JWT
acceptanceCriteria.then: the response status is 404 and the body matches the ApiErrorResponse shape

tasks.summary: Add JWT signing key to application-test.yaml for integration test token generation
tasks.description:
Add the following to backend/loan-service/src/test/resources/application-test.yaml so the security filter chain can validate locally generated test tokens:

```yaml
spring:
  security:
    oauth2:
      resourceserver:
        jwt:
          secret: test-local-secret-key-min-32-chars
```

This mirrors the dev profile secret approach already planned in SecurityConfig. Tests generate tokens using the same secret via a helper method.

tasks.summary: Create JwtTestHelper utility for generating test tokens
